help: It's okay that I'm resisting - but I can still start.
I don't have to want it to do it.
I can forgive the delay and begin anyway.
It's okay that I resisted - I can begin now.
I'm not lazy, I'm tired - and still capable.
I can show up imperfectly and that's enough.
perspective: Avoidance makes the weight heavier - action makes it lighter.
Starting is the hardest part - after that, it's easier.
Procrastination is just fear wearing a mask.
The weight shrinks once I start moving.
Perfection is a trap; progress is enough.
Work can be ugly and still count.
momentum: I don't need to do it all - just start.
Five minutes is enough to break the wall.
Starting small is still starting.
I can lower the bar and begin.
One step breaks the loop.
Perfection is not needed to begin.
I can be sloppy - progress matters more.
I don't need motivation; I need a micro-step.
I don't need to finish - just start.
Do the smallest next step, nothing else.
A tiny spark is enough to light the fire.
Starting imperfectly is still starting.
Even a crawl forward is still movement.
I can take one gentle step forward.
One line of code is progress.
Start messy, polish later.
Any action is better than none.
Done badly is better than not done at all.
I forgive the delay - now I move.
Starting late is still starting.
The hardest part is beginning - and I'm here now.
Every fresh start counts, even at 3 PM.
I only need to do this one thing right now.
done: I made it through this moment.
